content: AMR containment strategies: (1) Antibiotic stewardship—prescribe only when needed, select narrowest spectrum, optimize dose/duration; (2) Infection prevention—hand hygiene, isolation precautions, environmental cleaning; (3) Vaccination—reduce infections requiring antibiotics; (4) Surveillance—track resistance patterns, detect emerging threats; (5) One Health approach—reduce agricultural antibiotic use, improve animal husbandry; (6) Sanitation infrastructure—clean water, wastewater treatment; (7) Research incentives—push/pull mechanisms for new antibiotics, alternatives like phage therapy. National action plans coordinate multisectoral responses with measurable targets and accountability.
source: WHO Global Action Plan on AMR, CDC Core Elements of Hospital Antibiotic Stewardship
